challenge_text: Publish payloads with embedded newlines, NUL bytes, multi-KB size, a wrong/oversized length prefix, and bogus --at values (past, far future, non-numeric). Confirm no crash, no hang. Verify docs now honestly predict the newline-delimited NUL-free-text contract, and that bogus --at is rejected (not a silent server-side crash).
capabilities_used: ls deferred/, cat -A subscriber output, grep broker.log

friction_points.description: Exit-code inconsistency on missing required args. 'shellmux pub' with NO arguments prints usage and exits 0, while 'shellmux' (no subcommand) and 'shellmux boguscmd' both correctly exit 1. A scripter checking $? cannot distinguish 'I forgot the topic arg' from a successful publish via the exit code alone. The --help-style rc=0 leaked into the missing-args case. No data is published (harmless), but it breaks the otherwise-clean exit-code contract this persona relies on.
friction_points.workaround: Always pass explicit args; do not rely on pub rc to detect a forgotten positional. Pipe stderr usage line as the real signal.

friction_points.description:
DEMO/README say a payload is 'truncated at its first newline', but a multi-line payload piped to a single pub (printf 'line1
line2
line3
') is delivered as THREE separate intact lines, not one truncated line. This is actually friendlier than the doc text (no data silently lost — each newline becomes its own record), but it means the literal wording 'truncated at first newline' under-describes what a scripter observes when piping a multi-line file. The contract a scripter should internalize is 'one record per line', which is fine; the 'truncated' framing is a slight mismatch with observed behaviour.
friction_points.workaround: Treat each line as its own record; the truncation wording only bites if you assume a multi-line blob arrives as one frame.

wished_for: A documented note that 'truncated at first newline' really means 'split into one record per line' when a multi-line blob is piped to a single pub.
Nonzero exit on 'pub' with missing required positional args (distinct from --help).
what_worked_well: Bogus --at/--delay (xyz, 1.5, 99999999999, -5) all rejected with rc=1 and a single clean stderr reason line; broker stayed UP with ZERO raw bash errors in broker.log (F1 stays fixed).
Far-future --at 99999999999 rejected; deferred/ stayed empty even after 50 bogus pubs in a loop (F7 stays fixed).
Topic traversal '../../../tmp/PWNED' rejected; no /tmp/PWNED created (H1 stays fixed).
pub/sub --help print usage and exit 0 (F4 stays fixed).

NUL bytes stripped (abc\x00def\x00ghi -> abcdefghi), 8KB single line delivered intact, unterminated payload not delivered until a newline arrives -- EXACTLY as README/DEMO now predict (H2 doc honesty resolved).
Past --at 1 fired immediately; near-future --at NOW+5 parked a deferred file as expected.
flood_wedged.sh F4 explicitly asserts frame integrity under flood; passed twice (drops visible, max descendants 18/19 flat) -- H4 stable across runs.
Broker never crashed or hung under any malformed input I threw at it.
comparison_to_expectations: Round-001 rated this pair 4/10 because the 'content-blind'/binary claims were overstated and bogus --at crashed the handler server-side while the publisher got rc=0. Round-002 is a clear, verifiable improvement: the docs now state the honest newline-delimited NUL-free-text contract and I observed exactly that behaviour; bogus --at is rejected at the boundary with nonzero rc and a clean reason, with no bash error spew in broker.log. The two remaining frictions are low-severity polish (a missing-args exit code and a slight 'truncated' wording mismatch), not honesty failures.
free_text: Hammered the framing path as a scripter: embedded newlines (split into 3 clean records), NUL bytes (stripped to abcdefghi), 8KB single line (intact), unterminated payload (correctly withheld). Every doc claim in the 'delivery contract' section held up under cat -A inspection. The control-line hardening is real: 50 junk --at values in a loop left deferred/ empty and the broker up with a clean log. The only nits are an exit-code-0 on 'pub' with no args (inconsistent with the rc=1 on a bogus subcommand) and the 'truncated at first newline' wording slightly under-selling the friendlier per-line split I actually saw. Both are low severity. This challenge went from the lowest-rated pair to one I'd trust in a pipeline.

claim_audit.claim: embedded NUL bytes are stripped, a payload is truncated at its first newline, and a record with no trailing newline is not delivered until one arrives
claim_audit.evidence: NUL: 'abc\x00def\x00ghi\n' delivered as 'abcdefghi' (cat -A confirmed no ^@). No-newline: 'no-newline-tail' never appeared in subscriber output. Multi-line blob arrived as 3 separate intact lines (per-line split rather than literal one-line truncation -- friendlier than worded).

claim_audit.claim: --at/--delay must be a non-negative integer within SHELLMUX_MAX_DEFER_S ... a malformed or far-future deadline returns a nonzero exit with a one-line reason instead of crashing a handler or parking a never-firing record
claim_audit.evidence: --at xyz, --delay 1.5, --at 99999999999, --delay -5 all returned rc=1 with a single 'shellmux: bad --at/--delay ...' line; broker stayed UP; deferred/ empty after 50 junk pubs; broker.log had no unbound-variable/arithmetic errors.

claim_audit.claim: Topic names are [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ (no ../ traversal that mkdirs outside the state dir)
claim_audit.evidence: pub to '../../../tmp/PWNED' returned rc=1 'bad topic name'; /tmp/PWNED not created.

proof_assessment.notes: Not the focus of this challenge; did not run the chaos gate. flood_wedged F4 (frame integrity under flood) passed twice, which is the framing-adjacent proof I exercised.

dismissal_survived.notes: n/a for this product-phase framing challenge
